title: Pipeline strainer
abstract: A pipeline strainer having a body with a straining element therein. A portion of the straining element is in the path of fluid flowing from the inlet to the outlet of the body. A first end of the straining element may be parallel to the inlet, or may be coextensive with the inlet. The second end of the straining element may be disposed proximate a debris drain. A first axis from the inlet for fluid to the outlet for fluid and a second axis from the inlet for fluid to the debris drain may form an angle of less than 30. A rotatable plug may be disposed in the debris drain. The straining element may include at least one corrugation. A cover plate may be removable secured to the bottom of the body of the pipeline strainer.
claims:
Claims 1. A pipeline strainer comprising: a body comprising a Y shape, a cavity, an inlet for a fluid lying in a first plane, an outlet for the fluid, and a debris drain; and, a straining element for removing debris from the fluid, the straining element comprising a first end lying in a second plane and a second end, wherein the straining element is disposed in the cavity of the body such that the second plane is generally parallel to the first plane and the second end of the straining element is disposed proximate the debris drain. 2. The pipeline strainer of claim 1 wherein the first end of the straining element and the second end of the straining element are both open ends. 3. The pipeline strainer of claim 1 or 2 wherein the straining element includes at least one corrugation disposed between the first end and the second end. 4. The pipeline strainer of any one of claims 1 to 3 wherein the first plane and the second plane are coextensive. 5. The pipeline strainer of any one of claims 1 to 4 wherein the debris drain comprises at least one threaded aperture and at least one plug for each threaded aperture. 6. The pipeline strainer of claim 5 wherein the at least one plug of the debris drain further comprises at least one threaded aperture. 7. The pipeline strainer of any one of claims 1 to 6 wherein the first end of the straining element includes a flange, and wherein the inlet of the body 

 includes a flange with a groove sized to receive the flange of the straining element. 8. The pipeline strainer of any one of claims 1 to 7 further comprising: a first axis from the inlet for fluid to the outlet for fluid and a second axis from the inlet for fluid to the debris drain forming an angle of less than 30Â°. 9. A pipeline strainer comprising: a body comprising a Y shape, a cavity, an inlet, an outlet, and a debris drain; and, a straining element disposed in the cavity of the body, the straining element comprising a tubular body with a first end and a second end and at least one corrugation disposed between the first end and the second end. 10. The pipeline strainer of claim 9 wherein the debris drain comprises at least one threaded aperture and at least one plug for each threaded aperture. 11. The pipeline strainer of claim 9 or 10 wherein a longitudinal axis of the tubular body extends from the first end to the second end, the first end being planar and lying in a plane oblique to the longitudinal axis of the tubular body, the second end being planar and lying in plane substantially perpendicular to the longitudinal axis of the tubular body. 12. The pipeline strainer of any one of claims 9 to 11 wherein the first end of the straining element includes a flange, and wherein the inlet of the body includes a flange with a groove sized to receive the flange of the straining element. 13. The pipeline strainer of any one of claims 9 to 12 wherein the straining element is disposed in the cavity of the body such that the first end of the straining element lies in a plane generally parallel to a plane including the inlet of the body and the second end of the straining element is disposed proximate the debris drain. 14. The pipeline strainer of any one of claims 9 to 13 wherein the body further comprises a cover plate, and the cover plate includes the debris drain. 15. A pipeline strainer comprising: a body comprising, a cavity, an inlet for fluid, an outlet for the fluid, and a debris drain, a first axis from the inlet for fluid to the outlet for fluid and a second axis from the inlet for fluid to the debris drain forming an angle of less than 30Â°; and, a straining element comprising a first end and a second end, the first end of the straining element disposed proximate the inlet for the fluid and the second end of the straining element disposed proximate the debris drain. 16. The pipeline strainer of claim 15 wherein the first end of the straining element includes a flange, and wherein the inlet of the body includes a flange with a groove sized to receive the flange of the straining element. 17. The pipeline strainer of claim 15 or 16 wherein the straining element is disposed in the cavity of the body such that the first end of the straining element lies in a plane generally parallel to a plane including the inlet of the body and the second end of the straining element is disposed proximate the debris drain. 18. The pipeline strainer of any one of claims 15 to 17 wherein the body further comprises a cover plate, and the cover plate includes the debris drain. 19. The pipeline strainer of any one of claims 15 to 18 wherein the straining element is includes at least one corrugation between the first end and the second end. 20. The pipeline strainer of any one of claims 15 to 19 wherein the body includes at least one shoulder configured to maintain the straining element within the cavity of the body.
